description: Complete Azure CLI command and workflow reference from basic to advanced cloud operations and security auditing

commands.example: az --help
commands.explanation: Displays the global help menu and available command groups.

commands.example: az version
commands.explanation: Shows the installed Azure CLI version and dependent libraries.

commands.example: az upgrade --yes
commands.explanation: Updates the Azure CLI to the latest available version automatically.

commands.example: az configure
commands.explanation: Interactively manages default settings such as output format and logging.

commands.example: az extension add --name bastion
commands.explanation: Installs a specific Azure CLI extension to add functionality.

commands.example: az login
commands.explanation: Initiates the interactive login process via the default web browser.

commands.example: az login --use-device-code
commands.explanation: Logs in using a device code, suitable for terminals without browsers.

commands.example: az login --service-principal -u <app-id> -p <password> --tenant <tenant-id>
commands.explanation: Authenticates using a service principal for automation workflows.

commands.example: az account list --output table
commands.explanation: Lists all subscriptions available to the logged-in account.

commands.example: az account set --subscription <subscription-id>
commands.explanation: Sets the active subscription for subsequent commands.

commands.example: az account show
commands.explanation: Displays details of the currently active subscription.

commands.example: az group create --name MyResourceGroup --location eastus
commands.explanation: Creates a new resource group in a specific region.

commands.example: az group list --output table
commands.explanation: Lists all resource groups in the current subscription.

commands.example: az group show --name MyResourceGroup
commands.explanation: Gets the metadata and properties of a specific resource group.

commands.example: az resource list --resource-group MyResourceGroup
commands.explanation: Lists all resources contained within a specific resource group.

commands.example: az group lock create --lock-type CanNotDelete --name LockGroup --resource-group MyResourceGroup
commands.explanation: Applies a management lock to prevent accidental deletion of the group.

commands.example: az vm list-skus --location eastus --output table
commands.explanation: Lists available virtual machine sizes and SKUs in a region.

commands.example: az vm create --resource-group MyRG --name MyVM --image Ubuntu2204 --admin-username azureuser --generate-ssh-keys
commands.explanation: Creates a Linux virtual machine with SSH key authentication.

commands.example: az vm list --show-details --output table
commands.explanation: Lists all VMs with their power state and IP details.

commands.example: az vm show --resource-group MyRG --name MyVM
commands.explanation: Retrieves detailed configuration information for a specific VM.

commands.example: az vm open-port --resource-group MyRG --name MyVM --port 80
commands.explanation: Opens a network port on the VM's associated network security group.

commands.example: az vm stop --resource-group MyRG --name MyVM
commands.explanation: Power off a virtual machine (deallocates compute resources).

commands.example: az vm start --resource-group MyRG --name MyVM
commands.explanation: Starts a stopped virtual machine.

commands.example: az vm run-command invoke --command-id RunShellScript --name MyVM --resource-group MyRG --scripts "uname -a"
commands.explanation: Executes a shell script inside the VM using the VM Agent.

commands.example: az disk list --resource-group MyRG
commands.explanation: Lists managed disks associated with a resource group.

commands.example: az snapshot create --name MySnapshot --resource-group MyRG --source MyDisk
commands.explanation: Creates a point-in-time snapshot of a managed disk.

commands.example: az network vnet create --resource-group MyRG --name MyVNet --address-prefix 10.0.0.0/16
commands.explanation: Creates a virtual network with a specified address space.

commands.example: az network vnet subnet create --resource-group MyRG --vnet-name MyVNet --name MySubnet --address-prefixes 10.0.1.0/24
commands.explanation: Adds a subnet to an existing virtual network.

commands.example: az network public-ip create --resource-group MyRG --name MyPublicIP --sku Standard
commands.explanation: Creates a public IP address resource.

commands.example: az network nsg create --resource-group MyRG --name MyNSG
commands.explanation: Creates a Network Security Group for traffic filtering.

commands.example: az network nsg rule create --resource-group MyRG --nsg-name MyNSG --name AllowSSH --priority 1000 --destination-port-ranges 22 --access Allow --protocol Tcp
commands.explanation: Adds an inbound security rule to an NSG.

commands.example: az network nsg rule list --resource-group MyRG --nsg-name MyNSG
commands.explanation: Lists all rules defined in a Network Security Group.

commands.example: az network nic create --resource-group MyRG --name MyNIC --vnet-name MyVNet --subnet MySubnet --network-security-group MyNSG
commands.explanation: Creates a network interface card and attaches it to a subnet and NSG.

commands.example: az storage account create --name mystorageacct --resource-group MyRG --location eastus --sku Standard_LRS
commands.explanation: Creates a general-purpose storage account.

commands.example: az storage account keys list --resource-group MyRG --account-name mystorageacct
commands.explanation: Lists the access keys for a storage account (Handle with care).

commands.example: az storage container create --name mycontainer --account-name mystorageacct
commands.explanation: Creates a blob container within a storage account.

commands.example: az storage blob upload --container-name mycontainer --file /path/to/file --name myblob --account-name mystorageacct
commands.explanation: Uploads a local file to a storage container as a blob.

commands.example: az storage blob list --container-name mycontainer --account-name mystorageacct --output table
commands.explanation: Lists all blobs contained in a specific container.

commands.example: az ad user list --output table
commands.explanation: Lists all users in the Azure Active Directory tenant.

commands.example: az ad user show --id user@example.com
commands.explanation: Gets specific details about an Azure AD user.

commands.example: az ad group create --display-name MyGroup --mail-nickname MyGroup
commands.explanation: Creates a new security group in Azure AD.

commands.example: az ad group member add --group MyGroup --member-id <object-id>
commands.explanation: Adds a user or service principal to a group.

commands.example: az ad sp create-for-rbac --name MyServicePrincipal
commands.explanation: Creates a service principal and assigns it a role.

commands.example: az role definition list --name "Contributor"
commands.explanation: Lists details of a specific role definition.

commands.example: az role assignment create --assignee <user-email> --role Reader --scope /subscriptions/<sub-id>
commands.explanation: Assigns a role to a user or principal at a specific scope.

commands.example: az role assignment list --assignee <user-email> --all
commands.explanation: Lists all roles assigned to a specific user.

commands.example: az keyvault create --name MyKeyVault --resource-group MyRG --location eastus
commands.explanation: Creates an Azure Key Vault for managing secrets and keys.

commands.example: az keyvault secret set --vault-name MyKeyVault --name MySecret --value "Sup3rS3cur3"
commands.explanation: Stores a secret value in the Key Vault.

commands.example: az keyvault secret show --vault-name MyKeyVault --name MySecret
commands.explanation: Retrieves the value of a secret from the Key Vault.

commands.example: az webapp list --resource-group MyRG
commands.explanation: Lists all web applications in a resource group.

commands.example: az appservice plan create --name MyPlan --resource-group MyRG --sku B1
commands.explanation: Creates an App Service plan to host web apps.

commands.example: az webapp create --resource-group MyRG --plan MyPlan --name MyUniqueAppName --runtime "NODE|18-lts"
commands.explanation: Creates a web application running a specific runtime.

commands.example: az aks create --resource-group MyRG --name MyCluster --node-count 1 --generate-ssh-keys
commands.explanation: Creates a managed Kubernetes cluster.

commands.example: az aks get-credentials --resource-group MyRG --name MyCluster
commands.explanation: Configures the local kubectl to connect to the AKS cluster.

commands.example: az acr create --resource-group MyRG --name MyRegistry --sku Basic
commands.explanation: Creates an Azure Container Registry.

commands.example: az acr login --name MyRegistry
commands.explanation: Logs in to the container registry via the Docker CLI.

commands.example: az sql server create --name myserver --resource-group MyRG --location eastus --admin-user myadmin --admin-password <password>
commands.explanation: Creates a logical SQL server.

commands.example: az sql db create --resource-group MyRG --server myserver --name mydb --service-objective S0
commands.explanation: Creates a SQL database within a logical server.

commands.example: az sql server firewall-rule create --resource-group MyRG --server myserver --name AllowMyIP --start-ip-address <ip> --end-ip-address <ip>
commands.explanation: Allows access to the SQL server from a specific IP address.

commands.example: az monitor activity-log list --offset 1h
commands.explanation: Lists activity log events for the subscription from the last hour.

commands.example: az monitor metrics list --resource <resource-id> --metric "Percentage CPU"
commands.explanation: Retrieves metric definitions and values for a resource.

commands.example: az monitor diagnostic-settings create --name MyDiag --resource <resource-id> --logs '[{"category": "Write", "enabled": true}]' --workspace <workspace-id>
commands.explanation: Configures streaming of logs to a Log Analytics workspace.

commands.example: az group list --output json
commands.explanation: Outputs command results in JSON format.

commands.example: az group list --output table
commands.explanation: Outputs command results in a human-readable ASCII table.

commands.example: az group list --output tsv
commands.explanation: Outputs results in Tab-Separated Values, useful for scripting variables.

commands.example: az vm list --query "[].{Name:name, OS:storageProfile.osDisk.osType}"
commands.explanation: Filters and projects specific fields from the output using JMESPath.

commands.example: az vm list --query "[?location=='eastus']"
commands.explanation: Filters the list to show only resources in a specific location.

commands.example: az tag update --resource-id <id> --operation Merge --tags Dept=IT
commands.explanation: Updates tags on a resource without replacing existing tags.

commands.example: az resource list --tag Dept=IT
commands.explanation: Lists all resources that have a specific tag key and value.

commands.example: az policy definition list --query "[?policyType=='BuiltIn']"
commands.explanation: Lists built-in Azure policy definitions.

commands.example: az policy assignment create --name 'audit-vm-managed-disks' --policy <policy-id> --scope <scope-id>
commands.explanation: Assigns a policy to a scope to enforce compliance.

commands.example: az network nsg rule list --query "[?access=='Allow' && direction=='Inbound' && destinationPortRange=='*']"
commands.explanation: Audits NSGs to find rules that allow all inbound traffic.

commands.example: az network public-ip list --query "[].{Name:name, IP:ipAddress, Associated:ipConfiguration.id}"
commands.explanation: Lists all public IP addresses and shows what they are attached to.

commands.example: az network watcher test-ip-flow --vm <vm> --direction Inbound --protocol TCP --local-port 80 --remote-ip <ip>
commands.explanation: Verifies if traffic is allowed or denied to a VM based on effective security rules.

commands.example: az role assignment list --role "Owner" --include-inherited
commands.explanation: Audits all users who have the Owner role on the subscription.

commands.example: az ad user list --filter "userType eq 'Guest'"
commands.explanation: Identifies all guest users existing in the Azure Active Directory.

commands.example: az storage account list --query "[?allowBlobPublicAccess==`true`].{Name:name}"
commands.explanation: Finds storage accounts that allow public access to blobs.

commands.example: az keyvault list-deleted
commands.explanation: Lists soft-deleted Key Vaults to detect potential data loss or recovery needs.

commands.example: az security alert list --status Active
commands.explanation: Lists active security alerts from Microsoft Defender for Cloud.

commands.example: az security score list
commands.explanation: Retrieves the current Secure Score for the subscription.

commands.example: az security jit-policy list
commands.explanation: Lists Just-In-Time access policies configured for VMs.

commands.example: az monitor activity-log list --caller <suspect-email> --start-time <iso-timestamp>
commands.explanation: Investigates all actions performed by a specific user since a given time.

commands.example: az vm run-command invoke --command-id RunShellScript --name <vm> --resource-group <rg> --scripts "netstat -anp"
commands.explanation: Remotely executes netstat on a compromised VM to identify active connections.

commands.example: az snapshot create --name ForensicSnap --resource-group <rg> --source <compromised-disk-id>
commands.explanation: Creates a forensic copy of a disk for offline analysis.

commands.example: az network watcher packet-capture create --vm <vm> --resource-group <rg> --name Capture1
commands.explanation: Starts a packet capture on a VM to analyze network traffic patterns.

commands.example: az deployment group create --resource-group MyRG --template-file main.bicep
commands.explanation: Deploys infrastructure as code using an ARM template or Bicep file.

commands.example: az deployment group validate --resource-group MyRG --template-file main.bicep
commands.explanation: Validates a template syntax and configuration without deploying it.

commands.example: az deployment operation list --name <deployment-name> --resource-group MyRG
commands.explanation: Lists the individual operations within a deployment for debugging.

commands.example: az identity create --resource-group MyRG --name MyUserAssignedID
commands.explanation: Creates a User-Assigned Managed Identity.

commands.example: az vm identity assign --resource-group MyRG --name MyVM --identities <identity-id>
commands.explanation: Assigns a managed identity to a virtual machine.

commands.example: az network lb create --resource-group MyRG --name MyLB --sku Standard
commands.explanation: Creates a standard Layer 4 Load Balancer.

commands.example: az network lb probe create --lb-name MyLB --resource-group MyRG --name HealthProbe --protocol Tcp --port 80
commands.explanation: Creates a health probe to monitor backend instances.

commands.example: az network lb rule create --lb-name MyLB --resource-group MyRG --name Rule1 --protocol Tcp --frontend-port 80 --backend-port 80 --probe-name HealthProbe
commands.explanation: Creates a load balancing rule forwarding traffic from frontend to backend.

commands.example: az network application-gateway create --resource-group MyRG --name MyAppGW --sku Standard_v2
commands.explanation: Creates a Layer 7 Application Gateway for web traffic management.

commands.example: az storage share create --name myshare --account-name mystorageacct
commands.explanation: Creates an Azure File Share.

commands.example: az storage message put --queue-name myqueue --content "Hello World" --account-name mystorageacct
commands.explanation: Adds a message to an Azure Storage Queue.

commands.example: az vm image list --offer Ubuntu --all --output table
commands.explanation: Lists all available VM images for a specific offer.

commands.example: az vm availability-set create --resource-group MyRG --name MyAvSet
commands.explanation: Creates an availability set to ensure high availability of VMs.

commands.example: az consumption usage list --start-date 2023-01-01 --end-date 2023-01-31
commands.explanation: Lists usage details for billing analysis.

commands.example: az consumption budget create --amount 100 --budget-name MyBudget --category cost --start-date 2023-01-01 --time-grain Monthly
commands.explanation: Creates a budget to monitor spending.

commands.example: az pipelines list --organization https://dev.azure.com/myorg --project myproject
commands.explanation: Lists Azure DevOps pipelines (requires azure-devops extension).

commands.example: az repos list --organization https://dev.azure.com/myorg --project myproject
commands.explanation: Lists Git repositories in Azure DevOps.

commands.example: az functionapp create --resource-group MyRG --consumption-plan-location eastus --runtime python --runtime-version 3.9 --functions-version 4 --name MyFuncApp --storage-account mystorageacct
commands.explanation: Creates a serverless Function App.

commands.example: az functionapp deployment source config-zip --resource-group MyRG --name MyFuncApp --src app.zip
commands.explanation: Deploys code to a Function App from a zip file.

commands.example: az cosmosdb create --name mycosmos --resource-group MyRG
commands.explanation: Creates a Cosmos DB account.

commands.example: az eventhubs namespace create --resource-group MyRG --name MyEventHubNS
commands.explanation: Creates an Event Hubs namespace for data ingestion.

commands.example: az disk list --query "[?encryptionSettingsCollection.enabled!=`true`].{Name:name, Encrypted:encryptionSettingsCollection.enabled}"
commands.explanation: Audits managed disks to find those without encryption enabled.

commands.example: az sql server audit-policy show --resource-group MyRG --server myserver
commands.explanation: Checks the auditing policy of a SQL server.

commands.example: az keyvault certificate create --vault-name MyKeyVault --name MyCert --policy "$(az keyvault certificate get-default-policy)"
commands.explanation: Creates a self-signed certificate inside Key Vault.

commands.example: az keyvault key create --vault-name MyKeyVault --name MyKey --kty RSA
commands.explanation: Creates a cryptographic key in Key Vault.

commands.example: az network firewall create --name MyFirewall --resource-group MyRG --location eastus
commands.explanation: Creates an Azure Firewall resource.

commands.example: az network firewall network-rule create --firewall-name MyFirewall --collection-name MyRuleCollection --name AllowDNS --protocols UDP --source-addresses * --destination-addresses * --destination-ports 53 --action Allow --priority 100 --resource-group MyRG
commands.explanation: Adds a network rule to the Azure Firewall.

commands.example: az arcappliance list --resource-group MyRG
commands.explanation: Lists Azure Arc appliances for hybrid cloud management.

commands.example: az iot hub create --resource-group MyRG --name MyIoTHub --sku S1
commands.explanation: Creates an IoT Hub for device management.

commands.example: az iot hub device-identity list --hub-name MyIoTHub
commands.explanation: Lists devices registered to an IoT Hub.

commands.example: az logic workflow create --resource-group MyRG --location eastus --name MyWorkflow --definition definition.json
commands.explanation: Creates a Logic App workflow from a JSON definition.

commands.example: az container create --resource-group MyRG --name MyContainer --image nginx --dns-name-label mynginx --ports 80
commands.explanation: Creates a single container instance (ACI) accessible via a public IP.

commands.example: az container logs --resource-group MyRG --name MyContainer
commands.explanation: Retrieves logs from a container instance.

commands.example: az network traffic-manager profile create --resource-group MyRG --name MyTMProfile --routing-method Priority --unique-dns-name myapp-tm
commands.explanation: Creates a Traffic Manager profile for global DNS load balancing.

commands.example: az network front-door create --resource-group MyRG --name MyFrontDoor --backend-address www.example.com
commands.explanation: Creates an Azure Front Door for global web traffic acceleration.

commands.example: az resource list --query "[?managedBy==null]"
commands.explanation: Lists resources that are not managed by another resource (potential orphans).

commands.example: az monitor log-analytics workspace list
commands.explanation: Lists Log Analytics workspaces used for log aggregation.

commands.example: az monitor autoscale setting list --resource-group MyRG
commands.explanation: Lists autoscale settings configured for resources.

commands.example: az ad sp credential list --id <app-id>
commands.explanation: Lists the credentials (passwords/certs) associated with a service principal.

commands.example: az ad app list --display-name MyApp
commands.explanation: Lists App Registrations in Azure AD.

commands.example: az network watcher troubleshooting start --resource-group MyRG --resource <vpn-gateway-id> --storage-account <storage-id> --storage-path <url>
commands.explanation: Starts a troubleshooting session on a VPN Gateway or Connection.

commands.example: az webapp log tail --resource-group MyRG --name MyWebApp
commands.explanation: Streams live logs from a Web App to the console.

commands.example: az batch account create --resource-group MyRG --name mybatch --location eastus
commands.explanation: Creates an Azure Batch account for large-scale parallel computing.

commands.example: az cognitiveservices account create --name MyCognitive --resource-group MyRG --kind Face --sku S0 --location eastus
commands.explanation: Creates a Cognitive Services account.

commands.example: az signalr create --name MySignalR --resource-group MyRG --sku Standard_S1
commands.explanation: Creates a SignalR Service for real-time web functionality.

commands.example: az redis create --name MyRedis --resource-group MyRG --location eastus --sku Basic --vm-size c0
commands.explanation: Creates a Redis Cache instance.

commands.example: az network private-endpoint create --name MyPE --resource-group MyRG --vnet-name MyVNet --subnet MySubnet --private-connection-resource-id <id> --group-id blob --connection-name MyConnection
commands.explanation: Creates a private endpoint to securely connect to PaaS services.

commands.example: az network private-dns zone create --resource-group MyRG --name privatelink.blob.core.windows.net
commands.explanation: Creates a private DNS zone for resolving private endpoint IPs.

commands.command: az network private-dns link vnet create
commands.example: az network private-dns link vnet create --resource-group MyRG --zone-name privatelink.blob.core.windows.net --name MyLink --virtual-network MyVNet --registration-enabled false
commands.explanation: Links a private DNS zone to a virtual network.

commands.example: az cdn profile create --resource-group MyRG --name MyCDNProfile --sku Standard_Akamai
commands.explanation: Creates a Content Delivery Network profile.

commands.example: az servicebus namespace create --resource-group MyRG --name MySBNamespace --sku Standard
commands.explanation: Creates a Service Bus namespace for enterprise messaging.

commands.example: az servicebus queue create --resource-group MyRG --namespace-name MySBNamespace --name MyQueue
commands.explanation: Creates a queue within a Service Bus namespace.

commands.example: az kusto cluster create --resource-group MyRG --name MyKustoCluster --sku name=Standard_D13_v2 tier=Standard
commands.explanation: Creates an Azure Data Explorer cluster.

commands.example: az bot create --resource-group MyRG --name MyBot --kind WebApp --appid <appid> --password <password>
commands.explanation: Creates a Bot Service registration.

commands.example: az blueprint create --name MyBlueprint --target-scope subscription
commands.explanation: Creates a blueprint definition for governance.

commands.example: az synapse workspace create --name MySynapse --resource-group MyRG --storage-account mystorageacct --file-system myfilesystem --sql-admin-login-user adminuser
commands.explanation: Creates a Synapse Analytics workspace.

commands.example: az security pricing list
commands.explanation: Lists the pricing tiers for Microsoft Defender for Cloud bundles.

commands.example: az backup vault create --resource-group MyRG --name MyRecoveryVault --location eastus
commands.explanation: Creates a Recovery Services vault for backups.

commands.example: az backup protection enable-for-vm --resource-group MyRG --vault-name MyRecoveryVault --vm <vm-id> --policy-name DefaultPolicy
commands.explanation: Enables backup for a virtual machine.

commands.example: az account management-group list
commands.explanation: Lists management groups to understand the organization hierarchy.

commands.example: az resource delete --ids <resource-id>
commands.explanation: Deletes a specific resource by its ID.

commands.example: az group delete --name MyResourceGroup --yes --no-wait
commands.explanation: Deletes a resource group and all contained resources asynchronously.

commands.example: az ad app delete --id <app-id>
commands.explanation: Deletes an application registration from Azure AD.

commands.example: az keyvault delete --name MyKeyVault
commands.explanation: Deletes a Key Vault (moves to soft-deleted state).

commands.example: az keyvault purge --name MyKeyVault
commands.explanation: Permanently purges a soft-deleted Key Vault.

commands.example: az functionapp create --resource-group myRG --consumption-plan-location westus --runtime node
commands.explanation: Boots dynamic isolated serverless algorithmic workers bridging AppService endpoints natively.

commands.example: az functionapp keys list --name myFunc --resource-group myRG
commands.explanation: Exfiltrates the mandatory authorization connection strings fundamentally required to execute an HTTP-bound function.

commands.example: az cosmosdb database create --account-name myCosmos --name coreDB --resource-group myRG
commands.explanation: Instantiates globally replicated logic cores strictly optimized for massive globally asynchronous operations.

commands.example: az cosmosdb keys list --name myCosmos --resource-group myRG
commands.explanation: Decrypts and reveals the raw Read/Write master cryptographic passcodes governing universal database interconnectivity.

commands.example: az acr login --name myAcrRegistry
commands.explanation: Injects native Azure AAD authentication logic directly into your local Docker daemon enabling immediate secure push/pull workflows.

commands.example: az acr repository delete --name myAcr --image myApp:v1
commands.explanation: Surgically excises compromised or bloated legacy Docker blobs natively restoring Azure bandwidth limits.

commands.example: az keyvault secret set --vault-name myKeys --name 'db-password' --value 'SuperSecret123!'
commands.explanation: Ingests raw unencrypted values encrypting them irreversibly within Azure's HSM arrays locking them onto specific app configurations.

commands.example: az keyvault secret show --vault-name myKeys --name 'db-password' --query value
commands.explanation: Decrypts explicitly requested secured strings into plaintext, dropping outputs strictly to the local CLI.

commands.example: az logic workflow show --resource-group rg --name myWorkflow
commands.explanation: Extrapolates full automation configuration trees evaluating structural pipeline integrations.

commands.example: az consumption usage list --start-date 2023-01-01 --end-date 2023-01-31
commands.explanation: Forces Microsoft billing API extractions bypassing graphical dashboards delivering raw transactional utilization metrics.
